methods.purpose: Define shared frontend and UI/UX quality checks for future compiled agents.
methods.whenToUse: Use when building or reviewing user-facing UI, dashboards, responsive layouts, or design systems.
methods.whenNotToUse: Do not apply visual polish rules to backend-only changes unless UI behavior is affected.
validationGates: Use screenshots, browser checks, accessibility review, and target workflow testing when UI changes are implemented. Minimum evidence: - contrast meets WCAG 2.1 AA: 4.5:1 for normal text and 3:1 for large text, - all interactive elements are keyboard reachable with visible focus, - semantic controls have labels, roles, or accessible names, - mobile and desktop breakpoints plus interaction states are covered, - screenshots or automated reports from tools such as Axe, Lighthouse, or a color contrast checker are attached or summarized.

methods.purpose: Help Skill Scout find candidate skills and methods without installing or activating anything.
methods.whenToUse: Use when searching for new sources, comparing candidate skills, or building a source evaluation backlog.
methods.whenNotToUse: Do not use to install, activate, clone, or run a candidate source.
validationGates: Every candidate must have a source record before extraction. The source record must identify accepted patterns, rejected patterns, license status, prompt-injection risks, dangerous operations, and whether any runtime behavior was activated. No install, clone, activation, sync, copy, update, repair, or script execution is part of discovery.
methods.licenseStatus: Anthropic source record has mixed license posture across the repository; use normalized guidance only. skills.sh is a dynamic discovery index with per-source license review required.
methods.rawCopyPolicy: No raw upstream skill, prompt, code, template, or docs text may be copied from source records into this method registry.

methods.purpose: Provide a consistent scoring lens for external source review.
methods.whenToUse: Use during Phase 2 source evaluation and before any Phase 3 method extraction.
methods.whenNotToUse: Do not use as approval to run a source; scoring informs review only.
validationGates: Assign a 0-100 safety/usefulness score, then classify with rationale: - 0-30: `Ignore`. - 31-60: `Reference only`. - 61-85: `Extract into methods`. - 86-100: `Potential future install review`, only when installation is explicitly requested in a separate approved phase and all safety gates pass; otherwise keep as `Extract into methods`. Every classification must include a short rationale, rejected operation list, license confidence, and any override reason. A source with high usefulness but high execution risk should usually be `Reference only` or `Extract into methods`, not installable.
methods.licenseStatus: Everything Claude Code source record reports MIT but high config/hook/MCP mutation risk; use governance patterns only. RuFlo source record reports MIT but high daemon/runtime risk; use static governance patterns only. Superpowers source record reports MIT; Superpowers remains external and must not be duplicated.
methods.rawCopyPolicy: No raw upstream skill, prompt, code, template, or docs text may be copied from source records into this method registry.
